channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean president says martial law was an act of governance and denies rebellion charges
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's president defended his martial law decree as an act of governance and denied rebellion charges Thursday, rejecting the opposition-led impeachment attempts against him and investigations into last week's move.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: UN General Assembly demands a ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: The U.N. General Assembly overwhelmingly approved resolutions Wednesday demanding an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and expressing support for the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees that Israel has moved to ban.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call for intelligence assessment on ending U.S. support for Ukraine
item.description: Lawmakers want the key intelligence chiefs to assess U.S. security risks if America stops providing weapons to Ukraine and Russian wins the war. The lawmakers are pressing for a provision to be added to the National Defense Authorization Act, which the House is expected to vote on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Syrians flock to morgues looking for loved ones who perished in Assad's prisons
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — Mohammad Chaeeb spoke softly into his phone, telling a relative the grim news: He found his brother at the morgue. “I saw him and said my goodbyes,” he said. His gaze lingered on the blackened body of Sami Chaeeb, whose teeth were bared and whose eye sockets were empty.
item.dc:creator: HUSSEIN MALLA and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of tractors block central London streets as farmers protest tax change
item.description: An inheritance tax of up to 20 percent will be applied to British farms in 2026. Hundreds of farmers protested the new policy by blocking the streets of London with tractors Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican's keffiyeh Nativity scene raises eyebrows and then disappears — at least until Christmas Eve
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis presided over his weekly general audience Wednesday alongside a Palestinian-crafted Nativity scene that was missing its eyebrow-raising signature element: a keffiyeh-draped manger holding the infant Jesus.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration, officials said Wednesday, as President Yoon Suk Yeol’s office resisted a police attempt to search the compound.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US warns experimental Russian missile may be used against Ukraine soon
item.description: Russia could soon launch another attack against Ukraine using an experimental intermediate-range ballistic missile (IRBM), according to a U.S. official. The official said that Russia could fire its Oreshnik IRBM at Ukraine "in the coming days" in an attempt to intimidate Ukraine and its supporters.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: America's closest ally in Syria is losing ground as a new order takes shape
item.description: The jihadi rebels who toppled Syrian President Bashar Assad say they want to build a unified, inclusive country.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Krauss, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brazil’s President Lula to undergo a new procedure after initial surgery for brain bleed
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will undergo a new procedure on Thursday following up on an unplanned surgery earlier this week for a brain bleed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Rescue crews pulled two women from rubble several hours after a Russian missile struck a medical clinic in southern Ukraine, killing nine people and wounding 22 others, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron seeks political deal to name a new prime minister and restore France's stability
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French president Emmanuel Macron is seeking a political deal that would allow him to both name a new prime minister and “guarantee the stability of the country,” following the resignation of ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier, a spokesperson for the outgoing government said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania's pro-Western parties agree to form a majority government after political turmoil
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania’s pro-European parties agreed Wednesday to form a majority government made up of groupings that were traditionally on opposite sides and shutting out far-right nationalists who made significant gains in the election on Dec. 1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Brazilian beef and leather companies fall short in tackling deforestation, a study finds
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — The next United Nations climate conference, COP30, will be held in Belem, the capital of an Amazon region where widespread deforestation mainly driven by cattle farming has turned the surrounding rainforest from a vital carbon sink into a significant carbon source.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense secretary in Japan to support alliance as Osprey aircraft safety causes concern
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin met with officials in Japan on Tuesday to reaffirm the importance of their alliance and Washington's commitment to regional security as threats rise from China and North Korea. Austin also stressed that U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names former CIA official Ron Johnson as ambassador to Mexico
item.description: President-elect Trump announced Tuesday that Ron Johnson, his one-time ambassador to El Salvador, will serve as ambassador to Mexico in the upcoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Kimberly Guilfoyle ambassador to Greece
item.description: President-elect Trump named Kimberly Guilfoyle as ambassador to Greece in his upcoming administration. Guilfoyle is a former Fox News personality and has been a staple of the Trump family for years since she previously was in a relationship with Donald Trump Jr. The two reportedly have broken up.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Gabbard meets with senators amid questions over Russia, Syria
item.description: Former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, met Tuesday with senators on Capitol Hill as she faces concerns from Republicans over her controversial positions sympathetic to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Collapse of Syria’s Assad regime renews US push to find Austin Tice
item.description: The fall of the Bashar al-Assad and the mass liberation of numerous notorious prisons in Syria has brought renewed hope for the location and return of American Austin Tice, held captive for 12 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fall of Syria's Bashar Assad is strategic blow to Iran and Russia, experts say
item.description: Analysts and commentators say the collapse of Syria's Bashar al-Assad's regime is likely to reshape the power dynamics in the Middle East while signaling a critical shift in influence among world powers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Alleged Chinese cyberattack: FBI urges encrypted apps
item.description: Salt Typhoon, a Chinese government-linked hacking group, is one of the largest intelligence breaches in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation
